title: Peppermint oat foot soak
subtitle: A dry soak mix with Epsom salts, colloidal oat, and peppermint essential oil
excerpt: A dry foot soak blend made from Epsom salts, colloidal oatmeal, and jojoba oil carrying peppermint essential oil. Add to a basin of warm water. The Epsom salts soften the skin and relax the feet; the colloidal oat soothes irritated skin; the peppermint provides a cooling sensation. Makes 6 to 8 soak sessions.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): peppermint at standard bath-soak dilution rates. Colloidal oatmeal soothing properties: Kurtz and Wallo (Journal of Drugs in Dermatology, 2007).

content.text: A foot soak has two purposes: softening thickened skin on the heels and soles, and relaxing tired feet.

soften the skin and make a subsequent pumice or exfoliating treatment more effective. Colloidal oatmeal soothes the skin during the soak. Peppermint essential oil provides a cooling sensation and a clean scent.
The peppermint essential oil is pre-mixed with jojoba oil before adding to the salts to act as an
essential oil carrier in a soak

: this reduces the risk of undiluted essential oil pooling on the water surface and contacting the skin in concentration. Stir the soak water well before placing feet in the basin. Do not use peppermint foot soak on children under two years of age.

items.name: Epsom salts
items.prepNote: Pharmaceutical-grade Epsom salts. Available in bulk from pharmacies.

items.name: Colloidal oatmeal
items.prepNote: Finely milled colloidal oatmeal, not porridge oats. Disperses in the water and soothes skin.

items.name: Jojoba oil
items.prepNote: The carrier for the peppermint EO. Mix the EO into the jojoba first, then stir into the salts.

items.name: Peppermint essential oil
items.prepNote: 0.3% of total batch weight. Mix into the jojoba oil first. Toxic to cats.

content.text: Weigh the Epsom salts and colloidal oatmeal into the mixing bowl and stir to combine.

In a small dish, mix the peppermint essential oil into the jojoba oil. Drizzle this blend over the salt mixture, stirring as you go. Stir for 2 minutes until the oil is evenly distributed through the salts with no oily puddles on the bottom. Transfer to a sealed glass jar and label with the making date.

Scoop 60 g (about 4 tablespoons) into a basin of warm (not hot) water. Stir well to dissolve the salts and disperse the oil. Soak feet for 15 to 20 minutes. Follow with a pumice stone on the heels while skin is soft, then rinse and dry thoroughly between the toes.

Store in a sealed glass jar away from moisture. The salts are indefinite but the peppermint essential oil fades after 12 to 18 months and the colloidal oat can turn stale. The mix keeps well for 12 months. Stir before each use as the jojoba oil migrates to the surface of the salt. Discard if the oat fraction smells stale or the salt has clumped from moisture.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~360 g dry mix (6 to 8 soak sessions)
